full_prompt: Design the main hull (monocoque shell) for an eISR (European International Submarine Races) human-powered submarine, compliant with eISR24 Rulebook. The pilot operates on SCUBA inside a wet submarine (flooded hull). Geometric constraints per eISR24: overall hull length 3.0 m, maximum beam (diameter) 0.85 m (length-to-beam ratio 3.53:1, within rule limits), height 0.85 m. Hull is a one-piece GFRP (glass fiber reinforced plastic) hand-laid shell, [0/90/+45/-45]s quasi-isotropic layup with 0.5 mm plies, 12 plies total (6 mm shell thickness). Material: E-glass/polyester; E1 = 25 GPa, E2 = 8 GPa, long-tensile strength 400 MPa, long-compressive strength 300 MPa, density 1900 kg/m^3. Operating depth 5 m (pool); max planned depth 10 m for structural margin. Load cases: (R1) Hydrostatic at 10 m (100 kPa external differential, interior flooded, so net differential = 0 across the shell for a wet submarine -- the rule here is actually inertial + steering loads, not pressure); (R2) Pilot pedal effort: 500 N forward through the bulkhead propulsion mount; max fiber stress <= 400 / 3.0 = 133 MPa. (R3) Fin bending: aft control fins (deflectable by pilot) apply 200 N at 200 mm from hull centerline = 40 N*m moment at the fin-mount; max fiber stress at mount <= 133 MPa. (R4) Ballast release: mechanical dead-man releases a 5 kg ballast weight (5 x 9.81 = 49 N) from the hull; the mounting lug must survive 2x dynamic amplification = 98 N without permanent damage; max fiber stress <= 133 MPa. (R5) Linear buckling eigenvalue under external-pressure equivalent of 100 kPa (the wet-hull case is slightly more complex but model as crush check) >= 3.0. (R6) Positive buoyancy reserve: vehicle without pilot must float positively (rule requires at least +5 kgf buoyancy empty); displaced volume - structural mass - ballast <= -5 kg equivalent; geometric check. (R7) Hull structural mass <= 35 kg. (R8) SCUBA integration: clear internal volume behind the pilot's bulkhead >= 200 L for tanks and regulators; geometric check. Primary verification: laminate composite structural FEA for R2-R4 + linear buckling for R5. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 hull, laminate schedule, and a report listing hull mass in kg, max fiber stress per case in MPa, first buckling load factor, buoyancy reserve in kgf, internal volume in L.
short_prompt:
Design the main hull (monocoque shell) for an eISR (European International Submarine Races) human-powered wet submarine, compliant with eISR24 Rulebook. Pilot operates on SCUBA inside a flooded hull. Shell is a one-piece GFRP hand-laid laminate, [0/90/+45/-45]s with 0.5 mm plies, 12 plies total (6 mm thickness). Operating depth 5 m (pool); 10 m used for structural margin. Required positive-buoyancy reserve without pilot >= +5 kgf. Clear internal volume behind the pilot bulkhead >= 200 L for tanks and regulators.

Envelope: 3.0 m length, 0.85 m max beam, 0.85 m height (length-to-beam 3.53:1).

Load cases.
- LC1 pilot pedal effort: 500 N forward through the propulsion-mount bulkhead.
- LC2 aft control-fin bending: 200 N at 200 mm from hull centerline = 40 N*m moment at the fin mount.
- LC3 ballast release: 5 kg ballast (49 N) with 2 x dynamic amplification = 98 N on the mounting lug.
- LC4 external crush equivalent: linear buckling eigenvalue under 100 kPa (10 m margin) >= 3.0.

prompt.title: eISR24 human-powered submarine hull
prompt.brief: GFRP monocoque wet-hull submarine, 3.0 m long x 0.85 m beam, pilot-powered. Survives pedal/fin/ballast loads + crush buckling.

boundary_conditions.mounting: internal pilot bulkhead fixed for structural loads
boundary_conditions.loads: concentrated at propulsion, fin, ballast points

eval_coverage: buoyancy reserve + internal volume geometric checks
